title: 10 best herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for premenstrual syndrome are concentrated plant-based extracts used to alleviate the symptoms of PMS. These tinctures help by providing a natural and gentle approach to managing emotional, physical, and hormonal fluctuations. For example, Passionflower and Kava can help reduce anxiety and mood swings, while Chasteberry and Dong Quai can regulate hormone imbalances and ease cramps. By using herbal tinctures, women can experience improved mental clarity, reduced bloating, and increased energy levels, allowing them to better manage their daily lives and enjoy a more balanced premenstrual period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains phytoestrogens that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness. The tincture also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with PMS. Additionally, chaste tree tinctures may help to normalize ovulation and improve overall menstrual cycle regularity, leading to a reduction in symptoms and improvement in overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Diosgenin: A phytoestrogenic compound that helps regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms of PMS such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating by binding to estrogen receptors.
Iridoid glycosides: Compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety, stress, and emotional changes associated with PMS.
Flavonoids: Plant compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate physical symptoms of PMS such as cramps, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of PMS such as mood swings and anxiety.
Roots: The roots contain iridoid glycosides that help regulate hormonal balance, reducing symptoms like breast tenderness and mood changes associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried vitex agnus castus herb in large quantities from trusted sources.
Grind the dried vitex agnus castus into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 500 grams of the powdered vitex agnus castus with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least four weeks to allow for infusion and maceration.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids then store the tincture in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because its calming properties can alleviate symptoms of anxiety, irritability, and mood swings often associated with PMS. The herb's natural relaxant effects help to soothe the nervous system, reducing feelings of tension and stress that can exacerbate PMS symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's gentle anti-inflammatory properties may also help to ease breast tenderness and swelling common during this time. By promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort, herbal lemon balm tinctures provide a natural and effective way to manage PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Adaptogenic and calming effects: Lemon balm's adaptogenic properties allow it to interact with the body's natural stress response system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate PMS-related mood disturbances and emotional symptoms.

Musculotropic and sedative properties: Lemon balm's musculotropic properties can help relax smooth muscle contractions in the uterus, alleviating cramps and spasms associated with PMS, while its sedative effects can promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm are rich in bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as anxiety and mood swings.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source ensuring adequate quantity for processing.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in a warm air circulation environment for approximately two hours.
Chop the dried melissa officinalis leaves into smaller pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Soak the chopped herb material in a 70 percent vodka solution at a ratio of one part herb to five parts solvent for ten days.
Strain and filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth discarding solids and transferring liquid to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a natural ability to alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, irritability, and anxiety that often accompany PMS. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, helping to regulate emotions and reduce stress. Additionally, St John's wort has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease physical discomforts such as bloating and breast tenderness associated with PMS. By using a herbal tincture, women can find relief from these uncomfortable symptoms naturally, without relying on pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort's antioxidant compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with PMS symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Serotonin-modulating properties: The herb's constituents, particularly hyperforin, have been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and irritability commonly experienced during PMS.
Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause cramps, headaches, and breast tenderness during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hypericin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antidepressant properties, making it effective in relieving symptoms of PMS such as anxiety and mood swings.

Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce PMS symptoms like bloating and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at dawn on a sunny day, ensuring they are free of pesticides and contaminants.
Clean and chop the freshly harvested hypericum perforatum flowers into small pieces, using a sterile knife and utensils.
Combine 250 grams of chopped hypericum perforatum flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing for ample room at the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure maximum extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in the refrigerator for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural compounds that help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce water retention and ease cramps, while its adaptogenic qualities promote emotional balance and stability. Additionally, tree peony has been shown to regulate hormones and improve overall menstrual health, making it a natural and effective remedy for women experiencing PMS symptoms. By using herbal tinctures, women can find relief from the discomforts of PMS without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Tree peony tincture helps to alleviate cramps and spasms associated with PMS, as its antispasmodic compounds relax muscles and reduce pain.

Adaptogenic and calming effects: The tincture's adaptogenic and calming properties help to regulate hormonal fluctuations, reducing anxiety, mood swings, and irritability commonly experienced during the premenstrual phase.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-prostaglandin properties: Tree peony tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and prostaglandins (hormones that cause pain and cramps), helping to alleviate breast tenderness, bloating, and other physical symptoms of PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate physical symptoms of PMS such as bloating and breast tenderness.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidant properties that help soothe emotional symptoms of PMS, including anxiety and irritability.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia suffruticosa roots in the fall or early spring when they are at their peak potency for extraction.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine one part chopped paeonia suffruticosa root with two parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Steep the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure uniform extraction of active compounds.
Strain and filter the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with its ingredients and preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a regulating effect on hormonal imbalances, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The tincture's antioxidant properties also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to physical discomfort and fatigue during the premenstrual phase. Additionally, dong quai is believed to stimulate blood flow and nourish the reproductive organs, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and reducing symptoms of PMS.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties: Dong Quai tincture helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the uterine lining, which can alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS.

Hormone-balancing effects: Dong Quai has been shown to regulate estrogen levels and balance hormone production, which can help to mitigate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and anxiety that are common during PMS.

Antioxidant and adaptogenic properties: Dong Quai tincture contains antioxidants and adaptogens that help the body cope with stress and inflammation, reducing feelings of fatigue, irritability, and emotional instability associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins and polysaccharides, which help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with PMS.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain a variety of compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve hormonal balance, making them effective for managing PMS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at room temperature for 48 hours.
Combine 500 grams of dried angelica sinensis root with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the body. The tannins present in hawthorn help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness. Additionally, the adaptogenic properties of hawthorn help to regulate hormonal fluctuations, reducing anxiety, mood swings, and emotional turmoil often associated with PMS. By addressing these physical and emotional symptoms, hawthorn tinctures provide a natural and holistic approach to managing premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Cardiovascular tonic: Hawthorn's cardiovascular benefits can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common PMS symptoms, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.

Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's antispasmodic effects can help alleviate cramps, breast tenderness, and mood swings associated with PMS, while its anti-inflammatory properties may reduce fluid retention and bloating.

Adaptogenic and antioxidant activities: Hawthorn's adaptogenic nature helps the body cope with hormonal fluctuations, while its antioxidant properties protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like fatigue, irritability, and depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are known to reduce anxiety and stress, which can be helpful in managing symptoms associated with PMS.
Flowers: The flowers of the hawthorn plant have a gentle calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for addressing mood swings and irritability during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves at peak potency during full bloom.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and measure out 250 grams for the tincture recipe.
Combine the chopped leaves with 75% vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a ratio of 1:3.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter before bottling it in amber glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it offers a natural relief from symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and anxiety. The bioactive compounds present in elder flowers, like flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of irritability and stress. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of elder tincture can help alleviate physical discomforts like cramps and bloating, promoting overall relaxation and well-being during the premenstrual phase.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and fatigue associated with PMS.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture may help reduce prostaglandins, hormones that cause cramps, bloating, and mood changes during PMS.
Uterine relaxant properties: Elder tincture's uterine relaxant effects may help alleviate cramps by relaxing the uterine muscles, reducing spasms, and promoting a more regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as cramps and mood swings.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and other phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful in treating PMS-related inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Combine 500ml of pure vodka or grain alcohol with 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to release essential oils.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the final sambucus nigra tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, using 10-20 drops as needed before meals three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its ability to alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The herb's natural sedative properties help regulate the body's hormonal balance, reducing feelings of irritability and mood swings. Additionally, valerian tincture can also help ease cramps and bloating, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. By addressing these physical and emotional symptoms, valerian tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing premenstrual syndrome, providing women with a natural and effective way to feel more balanced and in control throughout their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Valerian tincture helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms of PMS, by acting on the brain's GABA receptors to promote relaxation and calmness.

Hormonal balancing properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a regulating effect on hormonal levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can help alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and fatigue associated with PMS.
Sedative and hypnotic properties: Valerian tincture's sedative effects can help promote a restful night's sleep, which is often disrupted by PMS symptoms like insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which help to soothe menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia.

Rhizomes: The rhizome, the underground stem of the plant, contains a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate PMS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots at peak potency during the plant's flowering stage.
Wash the harvested roots gently in cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Steep the root-vodka mixture in a glass container for 14 days, shaking daily, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a natural ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, alleviating symptoms such as anxiety, irritability, and mood swings. Its bitter properties also help to regulate digestion and ease bloating and cramps commonly experienced during PMS. Additionally, yellow gentian's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce water retention and breast tenderness. By addressing these physical and emotional symptoms, yellow gentian tinctures provide a natural and holistic approach to managing premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yellow gentian tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and joint pain.

Digestive stimulants: The tincture's bitter properties stimulate digestive secretions, which can help relieve symptoms of constipation and digestive discomfort commonly experienced during the premenstrual phase.
Vasoconstrictors: Yellow gentian's vasoconstrictor effects may help reduce excessive bleeding, cramping, and mood swings associated with PMS by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, which can be exacerbated during PMS.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for alleviating cramps and bloating associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried gentiana lutea roots in sufficient quantity for your desired yield.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and combine with vodka or ethanol at a 1:10 ratio by weight.
Place the root-vodka mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks.
Shake the jar daily during the infusion period to ensure all parts of the plant are exposed to the solvent.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date tinctures helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its unique combination of herbs that work together to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The tincture's blend of Dong quai, Angelica sinensis, and Chinese dates helps to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce water retention, resulting in a significant reduction in premenstrual discomfort. Additionally, the herbs have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe cramps and alleviate overall PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Herbal Chinese date tinctures, such as those containing dates, ginseng, or licorice root, can adapt to an individual's unique physiological needs and stress levels, helping to regulate hormonal fluctuations and reduce PMS symptoms like anxiety and mood swings.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The polyphenolic compounds present in herbal Chinese date tinctures, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like breast tenderness, bloating, and cramps.

Hormone-balancing properties: Herbal Chinese date tinctures, particularly those containing dates, have been traditionally used to balance female hormones and regulate menstruation. By normalizing hormone levels, these tinctures may help alleviate PMS symptoms like mood swings, irritability, and breast tenderness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, jujube roots help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as anxiety, mood swings, and insomnia.
Barks: The bark's high concentration of lignans provides relief from breast tenderness, mood instability, and digestive issues associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ziziphus jujuba fruits in the fall season when ripe and dry them completely.
Grind dried ziziphus jujuba fruits into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Combine the ground ziziphus jujuba powder with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to extract compounds.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and storing the liquid.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat premenstrual syndrome with herbal tinctures is by combining Chaste Tree Berry (Vitex agnus-castus) with Dong Quai (Angelica sinensis). These two herbs work together to regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce symptoms like bloating and breast tenderness, and alleviate mood swings. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture blend three times a day starting from the first day of menstruation until symptoms subside. This natural remedy can bring relief within a few days.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal tinctures are menopausal symptoms, perimenopause symptoms, and hormonal imbalances. Herbs like Black cohosh, Dong quai, and Wild yam are used to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, mood swings, and anxiety associated with menopause. Other herbs such as Chasteberry and Red clover have been traditionally used to help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce symptoms of PMS-like conditions in women experiencing perimenopause or hormonal imbalances.
